methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Reveal Technology is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Garrett Smith that has raised $30M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private companies and 2 public firms (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector, including peer companies Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $30M raised as a proxy for post-money value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $60M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M. The median across this peer set is $300M, with a range of $150M to $500M. The dispersion reflects material differences in technology maturity, market traction, or defense sector positioning within the cohort.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $51M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $180M. The wide range reflects two key uncertainties: the relative stage and maturity of Reveal versus the peer set, and the heterogeneity within Defense & Security valuations at Series A. Stage-progression methodology produces a conservative floor; peer comparables drive the ceiling but require careful stage-matching validation.
